questions.q: In which Indian state is the Somnath Temple located?
questions.explanation: The Somnath Temple stands on the coast of Saurashtra in the state of Gujarat.

questions.q: To which Hindu deity is the Somnath Temple dedicated?
questions.explanation: The Somnath Temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva, worshipped here in the form of a jyotirlinga.

questions.q: The Somnath Temple is renowned as the first of how many jyotirlingas of Shiva?
questions.explanation: Somnath is traditionally regarded as the first among the twelve sacred jyotirlingas of Shiva in India.

questions.q: Beside which body of water does the Somnath Temple stand?
questions.explanation: The temple sits on the shore of the Arabian Sea on Gujarat's western coast.

questions.q: What does the name 'Somnath' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Somnath' means 'Lord of the Moon' (Soma being the Moon god), referring to Shiva as protector of the Moon.

questions.q: The Somnath Temple is best known throughout history for being repeatedly subjected to what?
questions.explanation: Somnath was destroyed by invaders and rebuilt many times across the centuries, earning it the name 'the eternal shrine'.

questions.q: In which coastal town of Gujarat is the Somnath Temple situated?
questions.explanation: The temple is located at Prabhas Patan near Veraval in the Gir Somnath district of Gujarat.

questions.q: The sacred object enshrined at Somnath is a jyotirlinga, which represents Shiva as what?
questions.explanation: A jyotirlinga symbolizes Shiva as an infinite pillar of light, of which Somnath is counted the first.

questions.q: Somnath is one of the most important sites of which major Indian religion?
questions.explanation: Somnath is one of the holiest Hindu pilgrimage sites, sacred especially to devotees of Shiva.

questions.q: The present Somnath Temple was rebuilt after which country gained independence?
questions.explanation: The current temple was reconstructed soon after India's independence in 1947.

questions.q: Which famous Central Asian invader sacked and looted the Somnath Temple in 1025-1026 CE?
questions.explanation: Mahmud of Ghazni raided Somnath in 1025-1026 CE, plundering its wealth and destroying the temple.

questions.q: Which Indian leader and statesman is credited with driving the rebuilding of the modern Somnath Temple after 1947?
questions.explanation: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, India's first Home Minister, took the initiative to reconstruct the Somnath Temple.

questions.q: In what architectural style is the present Somnath Temple built?
questions.explanation: The reconstructed temple was built in the Chalukya or Maru-Gurjara style associated with Gujarat's temple architecture.

questions.q: Which President of India inaugurated the installation of the jyotirlinga at the rebuilt Somnath Temple in 1951?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad, India's first President, performed the installation ceremony of the jyotirlinga in 1951.

questions.q: The tall ornate tower above the sanctum of the present Somnath Temple is known by which term?
questions.explanation: The soaring tower rising above the sanctum in North Indian Nagara-style temples like Somnath is called the shikhara.

questions.q: The pillar at Somnath known as the 'Baan Stambh' (Arrow Pillar) symbolically points across the sea claiming what?
options: An unobstructed path to the South Pole
The location of a sunken temple
questions.explanation: The Baan Stambh marks that there is no land in a straight line from that point to Antarctica across the ocean.

questions.q: The legend of Somnath links its founding to which celestial figure of Hindu mythology?
questions.explanation: Legend holds that Soma, the Moon god, built the first shrine to Shiva here in gratitude for being freed from a curse.

questions.q: The Prabhas Patan area where Somnath stands is also associated in tradition with the final days of which deity?
questions.explanation: Hindu tradition associates nearby Bhalka Tirth at Prabhas with the place where Lord Krishna left his mortal body.

questions.q: Construction of the modern Somnath Temple was overseen by which trust set up for the purpose?
questions.explanation: The Shree Somnath Trust was formed to manage the reconstruction and administration of the temple.

questions.q: Which Delhi Sultanate ruler is recorded as having attacked and damaged the Somnath Temple in the late 13th-early 14th century?
questions.explanation: The forces of Alauddin Khalji's Delhi Sultanate attacked Somnath during their campaigns into Gujarat around 1299.

questions.q: Sardar Patel reportedly made his vow to rebuild Somnath while visiting the ruined site in which year?
questions.explanation: Patel resolved to reconstruct the temple during a visit to the ruins in 1947, the year of India's independence.

questions.q: The temple's location is significant because it lies on the sacred convergence of three rivers known as the Triveni Sangam at Prabhas, including which famous river?
questions.explanation: Prabhas Patan is revered as a Triveni Sangam where the Hiran, Kapila, and the mythical Saraswati are said to meet before reaching the sea.

questions.q: What was the approximate height of the central spire (shikhara) of the present Somnath Temple?
questions.explanation: The reconstructed temple's main shikhara rises to roughly 50 metres (about 155 feet) above the ground.

questions.q: The kalash (finial pot) crowning the shikhara of the present Somnath Temple is reported to weigh approximately how much?
questions.explanation: The kalash crowning the spire of the rebuilt Somnath Temple is reported to weigh roughly 10 tonnes.

questions.q: Mahmud of Ghazni's 1025 raid on Somnath is famously associated with a sandalwood gateway dispute later raised over gates kept at which Afghan city's tomb?
questions.explanation: The so-called 'Gates of Somnath' were said to have been taken to Mahmud's tomb at Ghazni, a story revived by the British in 1842.

questions.q: The medieval Somnath Temple was reconstructed in 1169 CE under the patronage of which Chalukya (Solanki) ruler of Gujarat?
questions.explanation: The Chalukya king Kumarapala rebuilt the Somnath Temple in stone in the 12th century after earlier destruction.

questions.q: Which architect-builder community/style guide informed the traditional plan of the rebuilt Somnath Temple, drawing on the work of Prabhashankar Sompura?
questions.explanation: Architect Prabhashankar Sompura of the hereditary Sompura temple-builder tradition designed the modern Somnath Temple.

questions.q: Before Mahmud of Ghazni's raid, the wealthy temple was supported by an elaborate system that included revenue from many villages and large numbers of which temple servants?
questions.explanation: Contemporary accounts describe the pre-1025 temple as immensely rich, maintained by numerous priests and temple attendants supported by village endowments.

questions.q: The present Somnath Temple is positioned so that, according to the Baan Stambh inscription, no land lies between it and which continent directly across the ocean?
questions.explanation: The Baan Stambh records that a straight southward line from the temple meets no land until Antarctica (the South Pole).

questions.q: The reconstruction of the modern Somnath Temple's main structure was completed and the temple consecrated in which year?
questions.explanation: The jyotirlinga was installed and the temple consecrated in 1951, with later phases of construction finishing in subsequent years.
